threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’ll spot them the moment they ask if there's a tube station nearby, clutching their map like it's 2003. Poor things.
Saw a guy on the 333 last night trying to convince a bunch of tourists that Brixton Academy used to be a church. Hilarious!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler playing with a stray cat while their parents argued about gentrification over shared vegan pastries at the café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a late-night Döner place that serves currywurst and fresh cocktails alongside your kebab. It's a wild mix.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they instinctively avoid the Civic Center BART station during a weekday. Loud bangs and smoke? No thanks.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 19 Polk this morning; he not only made it entertaining, but got the whole bus to cheer him on. Classic SF!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia’s plazas are just for Instagram. Locals use them for chatting over café con leche, not selfies.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still get lost in the narrow streets, staring at their maps like it's a treasure map, while the locals glide by without a second glance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think stopping at the corner shop for milk is quick; locals know it takes at least 20 minutes to haggle over the price.
Last night on the 35, a lad tried to sell a deck of cards to a sleeping woman. He nearly went for it when the driver shouted at him to stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy painting a mural on a wall while his dog laughed at pigeons. I thought, yes, this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to see spontaneous rooftop parties—everyone just brings their own drinks and good vibes. You won't find that vibe anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they know the BART delays like the back of their hand—while newcomers just look lost, waiting for a bus that won't show.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 21 doing yoga in the back seat, balancing on one leg. Everyone just pretended it was normal. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell but miss the real charm of Gràcia's hidden plazas, where the locals sip café and gossip under the trees.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them at the bakery, staring at the menu while everyone else orders without a second thought, clutching their Google Translate app.
